# primejob

> CLI that runs a local `uv run python train.py` on a remote Prime Intellect GPU pod with reusable dataset storage. The user keeps their local UX; the training executes on rented H100/H200/A100/B200/RTX4090 hardware.

primejob is a Python CLI (entry point: `primejob`, installed from `git+https://github.com/AleksanderObuchowski/primejob.git`; PyPI is not published yet). It is configured per-project in `pyproject.toml` under `[tool.primejob]` (`dataset_disk`, `forward_env`, `default_gpu`, `default_country`, `default_disk_size`, `bundle_paths`). Authentication uses `PRIME_API_KEY` from `.env` in cwd or `~/.prime/config.json` (written by `prime login` / `primejob login`). SSH uses the private key from `~/.prime/config.json` (`ssh_key_path`, set via `prime config set-ssh-key-path`) or `PRIME_SSH_KEY_PATH`.

When helping a user with primejob, prefer the SDK / CLI surface over guessing ŌĆö Prime Intellect's public docs lag behind the actual `prime_cli.api` Python SDK. Always run `primejob doctor` first to confirm auth, SSH key registration, paramiko, the SDK, and the `prime` CLI are wired up. GPU short aliases (`H100`, `H200`, `A100`, `B200`, `RTX4090`, `CPU`) resolve to full Prime SKUs (`H100_80GB`, `H200_141GB`, ...); `primejob gpus list` prints the full table.

## Core commands

- `primejob doctor` ŌĆö verify auth, SSH key (local + registered in Prime), paramiko, SDK, prime CLI
- `primejob login` ŌĆö interactive Prime API key setup
- `primejob dataset push ./data` ŌĆö upload dataset to the persistent disk (one-time, reused by every run)
- `primejob dataset list` ŌĆö list dataset contents on the persistent disk
- `primejob run <script.py> --gpu H100 --yes` ŌĆö pick cheapest matching offering, upload src (honors `.gitignore`), `uv sync` + `uv run python <script>`, stream output, download `outputs/`, terminate pod
- `primejob run <script.py> --gpu H100 -- --epochs 10 --lr 3e-4` ŌĆö everything after `--` is forwarded to the user's script
- `primejob run <script.py> --data-mode attach|stage|none|local` ŌĆö attach (default) mounts persistent disk; stage copies via helper pod; none skips disks; local bundles `--include-data` paths into src
- `primejob runs list` / `primejob status <run_id>` / `primejob logs <run_id>` ŌĆö inspect past runs
- `primejob attach <run_id>` ŌĆö re-open the Textual dashboard (view-only) on a running job
- `primejob terminate <run_id>` ŌĆö kill a wedged run
- `primejob gpus list` ŌĆö full GPU type table

## Runtime contract for user scripts

- The dataset path is exposed via the `PRIMEJOB_DATASET_PATH` environment variable in `attach`, `stage`, and `local` modes ŌĆö scripts should read inputs from there. Omitted in `none` mode.
- Anything the script writes under `outputs/` (relative to project root on the pod) is downloaded back to `./outputs/<run_id>/` on the local machine after the run.
- `forward_env` keys from `[tool.primejob]` are forwarded into the pod environment (typically `HF_TOKEN`, `WANDB_API_KEY`).
- Per-run state lives in `~/.primejob/runs/<run_id>/` (`manifest.json`, `log.txt`).

## Documentation

- [README](https://github.com/AleksanderObuchowski/primejob/blob/main/README.md) ŌĆö install, configure, full workflow, dashboard keybindings, dataset modes, GPU aliases, state layout
- [README (local copy)](README.md) ŌĆö same content, served alongside the source tree
- [pyproject.toml](pyproject.toml) ŌĆö package metadata, dependencies, CLI entry point

## Examples

Each example is a self-contained project (own `pyproject.toml`, `.python-version`, `uv.lock`, `README.md`):

- [examples/hf-sentiment-json](examples/hf-sentiment-json/) ŌĆö Hugging Face `Trainer` sentiment classification from `data/train.json`
- [examples/image-folder-torch](examples/image-folder-torch/) ŌĆö Torch image-folder classification from `data/image_folder/<class>/*.ppm`
- [examples/unsloth-sft](examples/unsloth-sft/) ŌĆö Unsloth + TRL SFT on a tiny in-memory instruction dataset
